title: Split FRAMEWORK.md into docs/ folder structure
note:
FRAMEWORK.md is getting large (200+ lines). Split into:

~/.openclaw/workspace/docs/
├── framework.md   ← Core rules (framework selection, anti-patterns, build tool rules)
├── roles.md       ← Agent roles, permissions, merge checklist
├── routes.md      ← Active routes manifest + testing procedure
├── stack.md       ← Tech stack, dependencies, gotchas
├── workflow.md    ← Dev→Prod workflow, automation rules, OpenCode usage
└── sources.md     ← External documentation references (SOURCES.md mirror)

FRAMEWORK.md becomes a lean index pointing to each file.

Move relevant sections from FRAMEWORK.md into these files. Update any references.

title: Browser automation testing
note:
Test the OpenClaw browser tool capabilities — can it navigate sites, fill forms, extract data, handle logins? Document what works and what breaks.

Scope:
- Navigate to a website and extract page content
- Fill out a form field and submit
- Handle a login flow (if possible)
- Test JavaScript-heavy SPAs vs static pages
- Document limitations and workarounds
- Write up findings in wiki/browser-automation-findings.md [auto-dispatched 2026-06-04 11:37]

title: Session cleanup cron + error spike watchdog
note:
Two related reliability improvements:

a) SESSION CLEANUP CRON — Create a cron job that runs every 6 hours to:
   - Find session files older than 24h with takeover errors
   - Archive them to logs/session-archive/
   - Alert if >5 takeover errors found in any single hour
   - Keep the session directory clean so monitoring tools work

b) ERROR SPIKE WATCHDOG — Create a lightweight shell script (not AI) that:
   - Runs every 15 minutes via cron
   - Counts errors in gateway log in the last 15 minutes
   - If error count > threshold, writes to data/alerts.json
   - Heartbeat picks up the alert and notifies Andre
   - Shell-only, no AI tokens burned

Both should be implemented as shell scripts + cron jobs, not AI agent tasks. [auto-dispatched 2026-06-04 15:37]
